_note: This is the scored context that feeds the editorial prompt builder. The actual prompt text is generated at runtime by src/domain/editorial/.

reasons: Calm winds are ideal for tripod stability and smooth exposures.
Mist or humidity adds mood that rewards slower shutter work.
Low-angle light can produce dramatic long-exposure colour.
Visibility suits the soft-but-present depth long exposures benefit from.
warnings: Heavy overcast may produce flat, featureless streaks.

reasons: Lighter winds should make subjects and longer lenses easier to manage.
Light S wind — plan your approach upwind of the subject.
Cloud-filtered light should suit wildlife contrast without harsh glare.
Visibility looks clear enough for longer-lens subject separation.
The broader weather pattern looks quiet enough for a general wildlife outing.
warnings: This wildlife score is a coarse scaffold without species timing, habitat, or scent context.

reasons: Calm wind suits long-exposure waterfall work with stable tripod positioning.
Humidity should create atmospheric mist around the falls.
Soft blue-hour light suits moody, silky-water compositions.
Visibility gives good scene depth around the waterfall setting.
Temperature and dew point are close enough for fog formation.
Light winds should help shallow fog or mist hold together.
Gentle SW breeze — mist may thin on exposed ground.
Cloud cover is excellent for deep-sky imaging.
Moon is well below the horizon — bright moonlight is not a factor right now.
Current haze and humidity look workable for transparency.
Atmospheric seeing looks steady for sharp star imaging.
Atmospheric haze adds depth to city scenes.
Blue-hour or night light suits moody urban photography.
Overcast skies diffuse light evenly across street scenes.
Humidity helps surfaces hold a reflective sheen.
Visibility should preserve depth and distant contrast.
warnings: Clear sky may lack the cloud texture needed for dramatic colour.
There may not be enough storm structure yet.
No precipitation, wind, or convective activity to support a storm session.

reasons: Wind speed is moderate enough for spray texture without it being dangerous.
Visibility should give good horizon definition and coastal depth.
warnings: No marine swell data available — seascape score cannot be calculated reliably.
